phase_1.title: Hierarchical Data & C4 Abstraction
phase_1.objective: Move from a flat file-to-file map to a nested 'System -> Container -> Component' model to eliminate graph noise.

tasks.action: Modify 'resolve_edges' to calculate weights between top-level directories (Folders). If Multiple files in Folder A import files in Folder B, create a single 'Aggregate Edge' with a weight attribute.
Enhance 'UniversalScanner' to extract class and function definitions using Tree-Sitter queries for Python and C++.
Update 'generate_html' to pass a nested JSON structure where nodes contain 'children' (files inside folders, functions inside files).

phase_2.title: WebGL Rendering & Semantic Zoom
phase_2.objective: Replace Canvas rendering with WebGL to support 10,000+ nodes and implement Level-of-Detail (LOD) visibility.

tasks.action: Swap the 'vis-network' library in 'HTML_TEMPLATE' for 'Sigma.js' or 'Cosmograph' to utilize GPU acceleration.
Implement a 'Semantic Zoom' listener in JavaScript: hide file/function labels when zoomed out; reveal internal components only when a folder node is double-clicked or zoomed in.
Apply 'Force-Atlas2' layout algorithms for better clustering of related modules.

phase_3.title: Agentic Navigation & Path Discovery
phase_3.objective: Transform the static HTML into a dynamic analysis tool where the LLM acts as a guide.

tasks.action: Add a new method 'get_path_explanation' that takes two nodes and their intermediary code to describe the 'Architectural Intent' of that specific flow.
Introduce a 'Live Mode' using a lightweight Flask/FastAPI backend so the browser can request fresh LLM analysis without re-running a full scan.
Add a 'Trace Path' UI component: users select two nodes, and the system highlights the dependency chain while dimming the rest of the 'hairball'.
